preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: SaaS trial users for AI-first, semantic design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: NavigationHeader is always eligible and sets the session context. Using the minimal variant strips distracting nav links and keeps focus on a single persistent CTA (e.g., 'Upgrade Now'), which is critical for Validator users at the conversion stage who need a clear, low-friction path to action without feeling lost.

sections.name: Hero — Conversion Argument
sections.rationale: Although HeroPrimary's funnel_stages don't explicitly list 'conversion', the brief's conversion context demands a strong opening. However, per Rule 3, HeroPrimary does not qualify (its funnel_stages are awareness/consideration/decision, not conversion). Substituting with CTABanner used as the opening statement — CTABanner supports both Validator archetype and conversion funnel stage. The Validator variant includes a subhead reinforcing the value proposition and a trust_signal ('Your trial data is preserved when you upgrade') to reduce friction at the decision-to-pay moment.

sections.name: Pricing Plans
sections.rationale: PricingCard is the core conversion mechanism, eligible for Validator archetype at the conversion funnel stage. The Validator variant includes detailed descriptions and longer feature lists, which satisfies the Validator's need to compare and verify before committing. Using the highlighted variant on the recommended plan and with-comparison for enterprise tiers gives Validators the structured differentiation they require to justify the purchase decision.

sections.name: Mid-Page Trust Reinforcement
sections.rationale: Placed immediately after pricing to address the Validator's post-sticker-shock anxiety. CTABanner in its split variant serves as a section bridge — directional copy on the left reframes the investment ('What you get when you upgrade') while the CTA on the right captures users ready to act. The Validator variant's trust_signal (e.g., 'Cancel anytime, no lock-in') directly reduces conversion-stage risk perception.

sections.name: Upgrade Confirmation CTA
sections.rationale: A terminal CTABanner at the page bottom serves as the final conversion capture for Validator users at the conversion stage. The Validator variant includes a reassuring subhead and trust_signal ('No additional credit card verification — upgrade with one click'). Positioned just before the footer, this catches Validators who scrolled the full page to gather evidence before committing — a characteristic behavior of the archetype at the conversion funnel stage.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal is always eligible. The with-cta variant is essential on this conversion-focused page, recapturing departing Validator users who may need one last nudge. Keeping legal_text visible also satisfies the Validator's need for transparency at the conversion stage — terms, privacy policy, and billing details should be accessible without friction.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.
Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). Centered layout works for hero positioning with messaging that addresses validator concerns about commitment.

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan drives conversion by reducing decision friction for validators evaluating options.
